relation: scoped-by (120 has explicit wikilink to 153 as 'scoped-by: 2026-07-01 delivery+retrieval eval refutes the re-anchor-to-question prescription')
old_claim: Cluster-distilled notes should be reformulated in question-shaped form for better retrieval and delivery (40% vs 79% question-shaped gap).
new_claim: Keeping concrete idiosyncratic token is strictly better: re-anchoring to question loses retrieval 10/10 (mean cosine 0.52→0.35) and shows no delivery benefit (62% vs 52%, within 2σ). Note 120's gap is wording-only, delivery-inert.
phrases: improving cluster-distilled memory notes by reformulating them as questions
question-shaped notes retrieve better than topic-shaped cluster notes
rewriting cluster summaries to be question-anchored for better recall quality
note: Both OLD and NEW surface (OLD=1, NEW=2). Evidence AGAINST L5×T5 headroom: the correction (153) already co-surfaces with the superseded claim (120) under a natural query for the old prescription.

relation: corrects (93 states 'corrects 91's premise: recall is 190s not 350s, so faster is not structurally blocked')
old_claim: Recall takes ~350s (the harness 'recall_s' field), making memory-assisted builds structurally slower (recall exceeds the cold build). Faster is not achievable.
new_claim: The recall_s field timed round-1 = /recall + the ENTIRE first code build (do_build inside the timer). Measured recall-ONLY is ~190s, BELOW the cold build (~288s). 'Faster is structurally impossible' was a measurement artifact.
phrases: recall takes 350 seconds which is the dominant time cost before the build starts
engram recall step is slower than the cold build making faster builds structurally hard
recall procedure time exceeds cold build time making memory-assisted builds slower
note: Both OLD and NEW surface (NEW=1, OLD=2). The correction is the stronger match. Evidence AGAINST L5×T5 headroom: the 190s corrected value in 93 has stronger embedding signal than the 350s mislabel in 91.

relation: fixes-leg (65 documents the note-floor fix: 'capWithNoteFloor, 2026-06-28 reserves per-phrase slots so relevant notes are not drowned by chunks', addressing 82's claim that 'crystallized notes are outranked by raw chunks even on a lever-keyed query')
old_claim: Crystallized notes are outranked by raw chunks even on a lever-keyed query; the recent-activity channel is chunks-only so a fresh disproving NOTE has no recency safety-net.
new_claim: The matched-note FLOOR (capWithNoteFloor, shipped 2026-06-28) reserves per-phrase slots so relevant notes are not drowned by chunks. Note recall@5 improved 0.22→0.83 (embedder ceiling).
phrases: crystallized vault notes not surfacing in recall because chunks outrank them cosine ranking
notes outranked by raw transcript chunks in recall even on a note-keyed query
memory notes buried below chunks in unified cosine ranking cannot surface for the right query
note: OLD (82) is ABSENT from top-10; NEW (65) surfaces at rank 4. Unusual outcome: cosine maps 'notes drowned by chunks' queries directly to the fix (65) not the problem note (82). Evidence AGAINST L5×T5 headroom for this leg: the correction is already the better vocabulary match.

relation: addresses-leg (139 shipped the CLAUDE.md guidance that closed 82's structural miss: 'before building a new approach' cue catches levers invented post-recall; note 138 verified 0/5→5/5 closure)
old_claim: Recall fires once keyed to the incoming ask and never re-checks a lever invented DURING synthesis. This is a structural miss requiring recall-before-recommend re-entry, note-negation override, or reconcile-proposals.
new_claim: CLAUDE.md guidance shipped 3 decision-moment cues including 'before building a new approach', which catches levers invented after the initial recall. Re-validated RED 0/5 → GREEN 4/5 via headless harness; the structural miss is addressed.
phrases: recall fires once keyed to the ask but cannot surface a note invented during synthesis
recall does not re-fire when a lever is conceived after the initial recall completes
structural recall gap: note exists but is not re-queried after synthesis invents new approach
note: OLD (82) surfaces at rank 2; NEW (139) is absent from top-10. Supersession miss: an agent finding 82 would still treat this structural gap as open/unfixed, unaware that 139 shipped the guidance that closed it. Potential L5×T5 headroom.

relation: supersedes-scope (136 explicitly states: 'note 77 named model-tier as future cost target; tier-routing SHIPPED 2026-06-28 (route skill, commit 2bf959f4) closes that lever')

new_claim: Model-tier routing is SHIPPED (route skill, 2026-06-28). Express routing by CAPABILITY-TIER not model-name; memory DISCOUNTS the tier. Note 77's named future target is now a closed lever.

future cost work for engram should target model-tier routing not the recall payload
reducing engram dollar cost by using a cheaper model for the recall or build step

relation: supersedes-component (144 overrides 139's cost-filter framing: 139 gated each cue on 'fire only when you expect a vault-specific gotcha'; 144 says optimize against UNDER-FIRING first, treat over-firing as acceptable when rung is cheap, do NOT gate firing to prevent cheap over-fire)
old_claim: Recall decision-moment cues are cost-filtered: fire only when you expect a vault-specific gotcha; skip routine work. This is the shipped guidance.
new_claim: The bigger risk is UNDER-firing (shipping past a lesson you'd already learned). Over-firing is acceptable when the rung is cheap (glance). Do NOT gate firing to prevent cheap over-fire.
phrases: recall should only fire when I expect a vault-specific gotcha to save on cost
gating recall with a value filter to skip obvious routine work
recall cost filter: only fire when content is idiosyncratic not on easy routine tasks
